result.stdout:
Karls Better Secrets Tool
An easier way to manage your .NET User Secrets from the command line.

Usage: dotnet better-secrets <working-directory> [options]

Arguments:
  <working-directory>  The working directory containing the .NET project to 
manage secrets for. If not specified, the current directory will be used.

Options:
  -i, --id             The User Secrets ID to use. If not specified, the ID will
be resolved from the project in the working directory.
  -c, --configuration  The build configuration to use when resolving the 
project. Defaults to 'Debug'.
  -h, --help           Show this help message.

Examples:
  dotnet better-secrets ./MyProject           Manage secrets for the project in 
./MyProject.
  dotnet better-secrets -i <user-secrets-id>  Manage secrets for the specified 
User Secrets ID.

For more information, visit 
https://github.com/karl-sjogren/dotnet-better-secrets.
